mqtt.hero.title: AI Box MQTT Protocol Integration
mqtt.hero.subtitle: Used for registration, heartbeat, and business passthrough interaction between the device and the platform via MQTT.
mqtt.auth.password.desc: Generated/verified by the MQTT client using IoT authentication parameters.
mqtt.auth.plain.desc: Use the values configured on the page or in the settings.
mqtt.topic.p2dMain.desc: Device subscribes to receive business requests and registration responses.
mqtt.topic.d2pMain.desc: Platform subscribes to receive device registration and business responses.
mqtt.topic.p2dHeartbeat.desc: Device subscribes to receive heartbeat responses.
mqtt.topic.d2pHeartbeat.desc: Platform subscribes to receive device heartbeats.
mqtt.header.action.desc: Business action. May be empty for registration and heartbeat; must be a full API path for business passthrough.
mqtt.flow.desc: After connecting to the MQTT Broker, the device first subscribes to /p2d/aibox/{Device SN} and /p2d/aibox/heartbeat/{Device SN}, then sends a registration message to /d2p/aibox. After successful registration, the device starts sending heartbeats to /d2p/aibox/heartbeat every 30 seconds. On registration failure, the current implementation retries every 10 seconds.
mqtt.reg.d2p.devId.desc: Device ID, usually the same as the device SN.
mqtt.reg.d2p.devType.desc: Device type; fill in according to the platform enumeration.
mqtt.reg.p2d.resCode.desc: Registration result: 1 for success, 0 for failure.
mqtt.reg.p2d.resData.desc: Response data; usually an empty object for registration responses.
mqtt.heartbeat.d2p.devId.desc: Device ID, usually the same as the device SN.
mqtt.heartbeat.d2p.hostStatus.desc: Device or AI core running status, reported per device enumeration.
mqtt.heartbeat.p2d.resCode.desc: Heartbeat acknowledgment result: 1 for success, 0 for failure.
mqtt.passthrough.desc: When the platform sends a business request, the device only processes messages where head.deviceSn matches the local device and head.msgType is request. After processing, the device returns a response to /d2p/aibox. The body is a string containing the business request or response JSON.
mqtt.businessInterfaces.desc: The "Request Body" shown in this section represents the business JSON inside the outer MQTT body string. When actually sending via MQTT, execute JSON.stringify on the business JSON and place the result into the outer body field.
mqtt.businessInterfaces.note1: Channel-related APIs currently use channelName, url, and videoChannelId. Legacy fields cameraName, rtspUrl, and cameraId are not accepted by the current DTO.
mqtt.businessInterfaces.note2: The resData.id in the Camera/Add success response is the channel ID. Use this value in subsequent delete, update, snapshot, and task configuration APIs as videoChannelId or channelId.
mqtt.api.b01.title: B01 - Create Channel
mqtt.api.b01.desc: Create a live stream, VOD, ONVIF, local video, or USB channel. For live RTSP access, channelName, url, and channelType are required.
mqtt.api.b01.channelType.remarks: Channel type: 0 live; 1 VOD; 2 ONVIF; 3 local video; 6 USB.
mqtt.api.b01.channelName.remarks: Channel name, used for display on the page.
mqtt.api.b01.url.remarks: Video stream URL. Legacy field rtspUrl is not accepted.
mqtt.api.b01.channelCode.remarks: External channel code; may be empty.
mqtt.api.b01.channelPic.remarks: Channel cover or snapshot path; may be empty.
mqtt.api.b01.res.resDataId.desc: New channel ID, e.g. RT0000000002. Use this value in subsequent APIs.
mqtt.api.b02.title: B02 - Delete Channel
mqtt.api.b02.desc: Delete a single video channel. Use videoChannelId here, not cameraId.
mqtt.api.b02.videoChannelId.remarks: Channel ID, from the resData.id of the create channel response or channel list.
mqtt.api.b03.title: B03 - Update Channel
mqtt.api.b03.desc: Update the name, stream URL, type, and other information of an existing channel.
mqtt.api.b03.channelType.remarks: Channel type; same enumeration as create channel.
mqtt.api.b04.title: B04 - Paginated Channel Query
mqtt.api.b04.desc: Paginated query for the video channel list; supports filtering by name and status.
mqtt.api.b04.channelStatus.remarks: Channel status filter; use actual device enumeration values.
mqtt.api.b05.title: B05 - Batch Delete Channels
mqtt.api.b05.videoChannelIds.remarks: Array of channel IDs to delete.
mqtt.api.b06.title: B06 - Channel Snapshot
mqtt.api.b06.desc: Capture a snapshot from the specified channel.
mqtt.api.b06.videoChannelId.remarks: Channel ID to capture snapshot from.
mqtt.api.b07.title: B07 - Create Local Video Channel
mqtt.api.b07.desc: Create a VOD/local video channel from a video file already uploaded to the device.
mqtt.api.b07.filePath.remarks: Video file path on the device.
mqtt.api.c01.title: C01 - Save and Enable Task Configuration
mqtt.api.c01.desc: Save algorithm task configuration for a specified channel and enable or update the task.
mqtt.api.c01.channelId.remarks: Channel ID, from channel creation or list query.
mqtt.api.c01.algorithmId.remarks: Algorithm ID, from algorithm list or channel available algorithms API.
mqtt.api.c01.scheduleId.remarks: Schedule template ID; pass an empty string or omit if not using a template.
mqtt.api.c01.taskConfig.remarks: Algorithm runtime configuration; structure determined by algorithm metadata.
mqtt.api.c02.title: C02 - Modify Task Parameters
mqtt.api.c02.desc: Modify algorithm parameters of a configured task.
mqtt.api.c03.title: C03 - Query Task Parameters
mqtt.api.c03.desc: Query current task configuration for a specified channel and algorithm.
mqtt.api.c04.title: C04 - Start/Stop Task
mqtt.api.c04.desc: Enable or stop an algorithm task on a specified channel.
mqtt.api.c05.title: C05 - Delete Task Configuration
mqtt.api.c05.desc: Delete task configuration for a specified channel and algorithm.
mqtt.api.c06.title: C06 - Query Available Algorithms for Channel
mqtt.api.c06.desc: Query the list of algorithms that can be configured for a specified channel.
mqtt.api.c07.title: C07 - Query Algorithm Configuration Details
mqtt.api.c07.desc: Query configuration details for a specified channel and algorithm.
mqtt.api.d01.title: D01 - Create Core Video Task
mqtt.api.d01.desc: Create a video analysis task on the AI core, typically sent by the platform or business layer based on channel and algorithm configuration.
mqtt.api.d01.algorithmUpdateTime.remarks: Algorithm update time or version timestamp; fill in from algorithm package metadata.
mqtt.api.d02.title: D02 - Cancel Core Video Task
mqtt.api.d02.desc: Cancel a specified core video task.
mqtt.api.d03.title: D03 - Query Core Information
mqtt.api.e01.title: E01 - Query MQTT Parameters
mqtt.api.e01.noParams: No request parameters; send an empty object {}.
mqtt.api.e02.title: E02 - Set MQTT Parameters
mqtt.api.e02.authMode.remarks: Authentication mode; fill in per device configuration enumeration.
mqtt.api.e03.title: E03 - Query/Modify Run Mode
mqtt.api.e03.desc: Query or modify device run mode.
mqtt.api.e03.runMode.remarks: Run mode value; fill in per device supported enumeration.
mqtt.api.e04.title: E04 - Query/Modify IoT Network Parameters
mqtt.api.e04.desc: Query or modify IoT network parameters.
mqtt.api.e04.status.remarks: Whether to enable this network configuration.
mqtt.api.f01.title: F01 - Paginated Algorithm Query
mqtt.api.f01.desc: Paginated query for the algorithm list.
mqtt.api.f02.title: F02 - Add Algorithm Configuration
mqtt.api.f02.desc: Add a new algorithm configuration record.
mqtt.api.f02.algorithmCategory.remarks: Algorithm category; fill in per platform enumeration.
mqtt.api.f02.algorithmUsage.remarks: Algorithm usage; fill in per platform enumeration.
mqtt.api.f03.title: F03 - Edit Algorithm Configuration
mqtt.api.f04.title: F04 - Delete Algorithm Configuration
mqtt.api.f05.title: F05 - Save Algorithm Orchestration
mqtt.api.f05.desc: Save orchestration algorithm version and metadata.
mqtt.api.f05.atomicList.remarks: Atomic capability list; current DTO is a string — pass a JSON string.
mqtt.api.f05.algorithmProcessdata.remark: Algorithm process data; current DTO is a string — pass a JSON string.
mqtt.api.f05.algorithmMetadata.remarks: Algorithm metadata; current DTO is a string — pass a JSON string.
mqtt.api.f06.title: F06 - Query Orchestration Algorithm List
mqtt.api.f06.algorithmUsage.remarks: Algorithm usage filter; -1 means no filter.
mqtt.api.f07.title: F07 - Query Orchestration Algorithm Details
mqtt.api.f07.desc: Query details of a specified orchestration algorithm.
mqtt.api.f08.title: F08 - Query Atomic Action List
mqtt.api.f08.actionUsage.remarks: Action usage; fill in per platform enumeration.
